rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) The proof of service filed on 10/25/24 did not show the complete title of the amendment that was filed on 10/9/24. (There were also TWO supplements, but notice generally does not need to be given for a supplement.) (2) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Is the bank statement an original? See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts of bank statements. (4) The time sheets attached as Volume Two, filed on 10/4/24, appear to be duplicates of some of those attached in Volume One. There is no total of hours or dollar amounts shown. (The attorney fee declaration, also filed on 10/4/24, has not been fully reviewed yet.) (5) The executor apparently just recently filed an ex parte petition, seeking authority to retain and pay an attorney in connection with the former tenant's lawsuit. Since this was already done, the executor should have simply disclosed this, apologized to anyone who should have gotten notice of such actions beforehand (or filed their consents to such actions), and sought approval of his actions from the Court, after the fact. (6) If the statutory compensation changes, should the distributions not change? (Executor had over $1,000,000 in one bank account. He apparently has not spread out the amounts since April, as he does not address this in his second supplement. Notice was served on the FTB on "July 16, 2029"?) Deadline is 11/26/24 to provide the assigned examiner a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any responsive papers. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY NANG TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 07/05/2024 and posted as tentative ruling for previous hearing on 09/03/2024) since this petition was initially set for 07/16/2024 and has been continued 3 times: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified by the petitioner. 4) Paragraph 7 of the petition: i) An allegation must also be made as to whether any heir HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b). ii) The date of giving notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be stated. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 5) Paragraph 10: Was notice of each proposed action given to the probate referee who requested special notice? 6) Paragraph 15: No summary of account was attached to the petition. 7) Paragraph 18: No Attachment 1 (schedule of proposed distribution) was attached to the petition. 8) Paragraph 19: No assignment has been filed as to Jacob Swaim's interest. 9) Paragraph 20 & Schedule A: If the $2,899.78 payment is a partial or full refund of selling expenses deducted from the proceeds through escrow, the statutory fee base receipts must be reduced by $2,899.78. 10) Paragraph 24: No declaration itemizing the costs sought to be reimbursed has been filed. 11) No schedule of disbursements was attached to the petition. 12) No schedule of property on hand at close of account was attached to the petition. 13) Financial account statements must be filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of original statements required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YAN PAL HUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/27/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/14/2024): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The word "executrix" must be removed from item 1 of the DE-120 form, as the petitioner is filing in her individual capacity as the decedent's surviving spouse. 4) Notice must be given to each executor named in the decedent's will, including successor executors. 5) Item 1a of the petition must be checked. 6) Item 1b must be unchecked, if the decedent held his 50% interest in each real property as separate property (which is alleged to have passed to the petitioner by will). 7) Attachment 7 must be filed. 8) If the decedent's 50% interest in each property was community property: i) Allegations required by LRSF 14.26(A) must also be set forth. ii) Item 7b must be checked. iii) Attachment 7b must be filed. 9) A copy of a statement of each bank account listed on Attachment 7a must be filed. The statement period must include the date of death. See LRSF 14.26(A). 10) Item 10 must list the name and address of each successor executor named in the will. 11) Item 13 must specify the number of attachment pages. Note: An appearance may be required on 11/27/2024 for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WENDELL EDWARD WILKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/24. (No earlier available date.) (1) The "Declaration" filed on 10/18/24 was an amendment to the petition. (2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) The amendment titled "Declaration" was not e-filed until 10/21/24, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website Even if it had been e-filed on 10/18/24, notice would have been insufficient for this hearing. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/26/24. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONG YEE CHIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The executor is the trustee of the devisee trust. Notice must be given to all of the trust beneficiaries. (2) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Elaine Chiu is only entitled to the tangible personal property under the will. (4) Paragraph 15 of the petition is untrue. There was $94,778.60 in cash as of the date of death. (5) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
